name: BENNETT RES (LOGAN & JULIE)
address: 1120 NW HILLSIDE PARK DR, BEND, OR 97703

fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Perimeter Fencing
fr.recommendations: To enhance responder access and operational effectiveness, maintaining a clear and unobstructed front driveway could significantly support entry for emergency vehicles. Considering the dense vegetation, trimming and managing these areas may improve visibility and facilitate navigation around the main building and landscaped sections. Identifying potential alternative access routes or ensuring that existing pathways are well-marked and clear could assist in addressing the limited rear access, potentially allowing for better movement during emergencies. Additionally, ensuring that the perimeter fencing has designated access points that are easily identifiable and accessible could assist responders by supporting efficient entry and exit when necessary. These measures may collectively enhance the staging and response operations for emergency personnel on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Dense Tree Canopy; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Shrubs Under Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Fuel Continuity; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Limited Defensible Space; Tree Cluster; Vegetated Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space around the property could be achieved by increasing the separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation as well as reducing the density of shrubs under trees to interrupt fuel continuity. Enhancing the separation of scattered trees and managing the dense tree canopy could further reduce opportunities for fire to spread. Addressing vegetation adjacent to the access route and near the perimeter of the property may improve firefighter access and support suppression efforts. By thinning the cluster of trees and managing vegetation along the access route, access routes could be better maintained, providing clearer pathways for emergency responders. These actions can collectively support long-term wildfire resilience and readiness.
